title: Ctenitis bivestita
intro: Ctenitis bivestita, also known as the two-veined ctenitis or two-veined wood fern, is a species of fern native to tropical Asia. The plant is valued for its traditional medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues such as arthritis, fever, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Ctenitis bivestita include flavonoids, triterpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Ctenitis bivestita is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including respiratory issues and skin problems.
taxonomy: Ctenitis bivestita is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This species belongs to the genus Ctenitis, which comprises over 100 species of ferns. The family Dryopteridaceae is a part of the larger division Polypodiidae, which consists of over 10,000 species of ferns and their allies. Ctenitis bivestita is native to Southeast Asia and is commonly found in tropical forests. It is a vascular plant that reproduces via spores, characteristic of the ferns within the division Polypodiophyta.

These compounds have been found to exhibit various bioactivities, such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties.
